text:
```sql
cloud=> select id, business_unit_id, created_at, status from interests_interest where id = 'e8889eb0-602f-4f66-8106-a54d802a2f42';
                  id                  |           business_unit_id           |          created_at           |    status
--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------+-------------------------------+---------------
 e8889eb0-602f-4f66-8106-a54d802a2f42 | eae45144-56c3-4899-8cc0-4b32079d40f3 | 2024-11-12 15:04:43.865222+00 | calc_accepted
```

give me SQL to get 20 rows before and after a specifc row with ID 'e8889eb0-602f-4f66-8106-a54d802a2f42', where the business unit id = eae45144-56c3-4899-8cc0-4b32079d40f3 and status is in either of these:

replaced
calc_accepted
in_dialog
offer_created
offer_accepted
lost
won


Here's a SQL query that will fetch 20 rows before and 20 rows after your specified row, based on created_at timestamp, for the given business unit and statuses:

```sql
WITH target_row AS (
    SELECT created_at 
    FROM interests_interest 
    WHERE id = 'e8889eb0-602f-4f66-8106-a54d802a2f42'
)
(
    SELECT id, business_unit_id, created_at, status, customer_name, phone_number, email
    FROM interests_interest
    WHERE business_unit_id = 'eae45144-56c3-4899-8cc0-4b32079d40f3'
    AND status IN ('replaced', 'calc_accepted', 'in_dialog', 'offer_created', 'offer_accepted', 'lost', 'won')
    AND created_at < (SELECT created_at FROM target_row)
    ORDER BY created_at DESC
    LIMIT 20
)
UNION ALL
SELECT id, business_unit_id, created_at, status, customer_name, phone_number, email
FROM interests_interest
WHERE id = 'e8889eb0-602f-4f66-8106-a54d802a2f42'
UNION ALL
(
    SELECT id, business_unit_id, created_at, status, customer_name, phone_number, email
    FROM interests_interest
    WHERE business_unit_id = 'eae45144-56c3-4899-8cc0-4b32079d40f3'
    AND status IN ('replaced', 'calc_accepted', 'in_dialog', 'offer_created', 'offer_accepted', 'lost', 'won')
    AND created_at > (SELECT created_at FROM target_row)
    ORDER BY created_at ASC
    LIMIT 20
)
ORDER BY created_at;
```

This query will:
1. Get up to 20 rows before the target row
2. Include the target row itself
3. Get up to 20 rows after the target row
4. Filter for the specified business unit and statuses
5. Order everything by created_at

The result will show at most 41 rows (20 before + 1 target + 20 after) in chronological order.
I want to print this as CSV
Here's how you can output the same query as CSV in PostgreSQL using the `COPY` command:

```sql
COPY (
    WITH target_row AS (
        SELECT created_at 
        FROM interests_interest 
        WHERE id = 'e8889eb0-602f-4f66-8106-a54d802a2f42'
    )
    (
        SELECT id, business_unit_id, created_at, status, customer_name, phone_number, email
        FROM interests_interest
        WHERE business_unit_id = 'eae45144-56c3-4899-8cc0-4b32079d40f3'
        AND status IN ('replaced', 'calc_accepted', 'in_dialog', 'offer_created', 'offer_accepted', 'lost', 'won')
        AND created_at < (SELECT created_at FROM target_row)
        ORDER BY created_at DESC
        LIMIT 20
    )
    UNION ALL
    SELECT id, business_unit_id, created_at, status, customer_name, phone_number, email
    FROM interests_interest
    WHERE id = 'e8889eb0-602f-4f66-8106-a54d802a2f42'
    UNION ALL
    (
        SELECT id, business_unit_id, created_at, status, customer_name, phone_number, email
        FROM interests_interest
        WHERE business_unit_id = 'eae45144-56c3-4899-8cc0-4b32079d40f3'
        AND status IN ('replaced', 'calc_accepted', 'in_dialog', 'offer_created', 'offer_accepted', 'lost', 'won')
        AND created_at > (SELECT created_at FROM target_row)
        ORDER BY created_at ASC
        LIMIT 20
    )
    ORDER BY created_at
) TO STDOUT WITH CSV HEADER;
```

If you're using psql command line client, you can also redirect the output to a file:

```bash
\copy (
    /* same query as above */
) TO 'output.csv' WITH CSV HEADER;
```

Or if you want to execute this from the command line:

```bash
psql -d your_database -c "\COPY (/* query here */) TO 'output.csv' WITH CSV HEADER;"
```

This will create a CSV file with headers and all the data from your query.
